### Comparison of Flour Milling Methods

| Feature | Tidewheel | Emberforge |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Mechanism** | Tidal energy (slow) | Steam engine (fast) |
| **Flour Type** | Coarse wholemeal | Fine, even white flour |
| **Capacity** | Low; limited by tidal cycle | High; operates 24/7 |
| **Labor** | Low (1 person) | High (4 people) |
| **Fuel/Resources**| None | Coal-dependent |
| **Maintenance** | Minimal | High (seasonal overhaul) |

#### Trade-offs

*   **Tidewheel:** Offers a highly sustainable, low-cost operation with minimal labor and maintenance needs. The primary trade-offs are significantly lower production capacity, dependence on tidal cycles, and a coarser product.
*   **Emberforge:** Provides high production capacity and a superior, finer flour product. The trade-offs are substantial: it requires a dedicated crew, constant fuel consumption (coal), and frequent, intensive maintenance to keep the steam engine operational.
